Uckfield Town Council is the local governing body for Uckfield, East Sussex, England. Composed of 15 councillors elected from five wards, it is currently led by Mayor Jackie Love and Deputy Mayor Donna French. The council's political makeup includes TRUST Independents (8), Labour (3), Conservatives (2), and Liberal Democrats (2). Elections are held every four years, with the most recent in 2019. The council meets at the Civic Centre on Bellfarm Lane.

Fauna in Armenia is diverse given the country's relatively small geographic size, owing to the varied habitats created by the area's mountainous terrain. Armenia is an important area for migratory animals, about 350 different bird species were recorded in the country. Many of the world's domesticated animals originated in the area Armenia is located in, and the mouflon, the ancestor of domesticated sheep, is present there. Research suggests that about a quarter of the animal species in Armenia are internationally endangered. The mouflon are suffering a great population decline due to poaching and habitat loss, and the Sevan trout, which made up thirty percent of the fish in Lake Sevan, have virtually disappeared.

Mutational meltdown is a process in which a small population accumulates deleterious mutations, leading to declining fitness and eventual extinction. As mutations fixate in the population, birth rates drop while death rates rise, causing a downward spiral in population size. This effect is more severe in asexual populations due to the lack of genetic recombination, allowing mutations to spread rapidly. In contrast, sexual populations may resist meltdown longer due to genetic diversity from recombination, though small or bottlenecked populations remain vulnerable. Factors such as low birth rates and strong mutation-selection pressures can accelerate meltdown, while increased reproduction may mitigate it. The process highlights the role of genetic drift in driving small populations toward extinction.

Alan Bristow (3 September 1923 – 26 April 2009) was a British aviator, entrepreneur, and founder of Bristow Helicopters. A pioneering helicopter pilot, he served in World War II, later establishing a global helicopter services company for oil exploration. He was involved in the Westland Affair and chaired British United Airways.

Jatavarman Sundara Pandyan I (r. 1251–1268 CE) was a powerful emperor of the Pandyan dynasty in medieval South India. He expanded his empire by defeating the Cholas, Cheras, Hoysalas, and Kadavas, while also launching successful invasions of Sri Lanka. Known for his military conquests, he extended Pandyan rule from Nellore to Kanchipuram. A patron of Hindu temples, he gold-plated shrines in Chidambaram, Srirangam, and Tirumala. His reign marked the peak of Pandyan power before his son, Maravarman Kulasekara Pandyan I, succeeded him.

Joseph Alexander Heller (born April 10, 1941 in Sydney, Australia) is an Israeli zoologist-malacologist. Heller is an Associate Professor Emeritus at the Department of Ecology, Evolution and Behavior in the Silverman Institute of Life Sciences, Faculty of Science, at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem.

Heller immigrated to Israel in 1949. He received his B.Sc. in Life Science; Zoology and Microbiology in 1965, MSc in Zoology in 1968 and his PhD in 1972 at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem. His dissertation thesis is titled "Studies on the systematics distribution and ecology of the landsnail Buliminus in Israel".

Heller was a visiting scientist at the University of Liverpool, University of Bristol, University of Cape Town, and the State University of New York at Stony Brook. He published 4 books and over 100 articles. His books are the first ever on the land snails of Israel and on marine molluscs of the land of Israel.
The Light Helicopter Experimental (LHX) program was a 1980s United States Army helicopter procurement project to replace the AH-1 Cobra and OH-58 Kiowa helicopters.

Tauwharenīkau is a rural locality in the South Wairarapa District of New Zealand's Wellington Region. It covers an area of 337.71 km² (130.39 sq mi) and had an estimated population of 1,500 as of June 2022, with a population density of 4.4 people per km². According to the 2018 census, the population was 1,353, with a median age of 47.5 years—significantly older than the national median. The majority of residents identified as European/Pākehā (92.5%), while 8.0% identified as Māori. Over half (50.8%) reported no religious affiliation, and 39.0% identified as Christian. The median income was $37,200, higher than the national median. The area has seen steady population growth, increasing by 39.2% since 2006.

Woodall services is a motorway service station on the M1 motorway in Rotherham close to Sheffield in England. It lies between junctions 30 and 31. It was opened in 1968 by Trust House Forte but was renamed Welcome Break after the takeover of the company. It takes its name from the nearby village of Woodall.

Woo In-hee (Korean: 우인희, died 1981) was a North Korean actress and a mistress of Kim Jong-il.

Having reached stardom in the 1960s and 1970, Woo, renowned for her beauty, acted in dozens of films. She married Yoo Hosun, a famed film director, but she was involved in affairs outside of her marriage. In the late 1970s, she became a secret mistress of Kim Jong-il. After Woo began an affair with another man, Kim had her publicly executed in front of 6,000 people.
Todd Scott is a New Zealand businessman, former radio host, and television presenter, best known as the owner of *National Business Review*.
Nashipur is a village in Murshidabad district, West Bengal, India, known for its historic Nashipur Rajbari palace and the Nashipur Akhara.

The pluperfect (or past perfect) is a grammatical tense used to describe an action that was completed before another past action or time. It combines past tense with perfect aspect, indicating that an event had already occurred prior to a reference point in the past. In English, it is formed with the auxiliary verb *had* and the past participle (e.g., *had finished*).  

The pluperfect exists in many languages, often formed through verb inflection (as in Latin) or auxiliary verbs (as in French, Spanish, and German). Some languages, like Romanian and Ukrainian, preserve synthetic pluperfect forms, while others, such as Slavic languages, often use alternative constructions. The tense is used for hypothetical statements, reported speech, and sequences of past events. Variations include the past perfect continuous (*had been doing*) and regional forms like the French *surcomposé* or German *doppeltes Perfekt*.

Caguas (IPA-es, IPA-es) is a city and municipality of Puerto Rico located in the Central Mountain Range of Puerto Rico, south of San Juan and Trujillo Alto, west of Gurabo and San Lorenzo, and east of Aguas Buenas, Cidra, and Cayey. Caguas was founded in 1775. The municipality had a population of 127,244 at the 2020 census.

Caguas is located 20 miles (32 km) from San Juan. It is located in the Valle de Caguas or Caguas Valley (also known as Valle del Turabo or the Turabo Valley), at the eastern ranges of the Central Mountain Range. It is known as La Ciudad Criolla (Criollo City), Valle del Turabo (the Turabo Valley) and La Cuna de los Trovadores (The Cradle of the Trovadores). Its name originates from the Taíno cacique Caguax, who was a regional chief. Caguas is a principal city of both the San Juan-Caguas-Guaynabo Metropolitan Statistical Area and the San Juan-Caguas-Fajardo Combined Statistical Area.

Ruth Ella Moore (May 19, 1903 – July 19, 1994) was an American bacteriologist and microbiologist who became the first African-American woman to earn a Ph.D. in the natural sciences. She received her doctorate in bacteriology from Ohio State University in 1933, where her research focused on *Mycobacterium tuberculosis*. Moore later joined Howard University as a professor and became the first woman to chair its bacteriology department, which she renamed the Department of Microbiology.  

Her scientific contributions included studies on blood types, dental caries, and antibiotic resistance in gut microbiomes. She was also the first African-American member of the American Society for Microbiology. Beyond her scientific career, Moore was an accomplished fashion designer, creating her own garments. She received multiple honors, including honorary degrees and recognition from Ohio State University. Moore died in 1994 at the age of 91.
Gilad Efrat (Hebrew: גלעד אפרת; born 1969) is a leading contemporary Israeli painter and a professor at the Shenkar College of Engineering and Design, Israel.

George Ege (March 9, 1748 – December 14, 1829) was an American Federalist politician, iron industrialist, and slaveholder who served in the U.S. House of Representatives from Pennsylvania (1796–1797).
Mouse-Warming is a 1952 Warner Bros. Looney Tunes short directed by Chuck Jones. The cartoon was released on September 8, 1952 and stars Claude Cat in his final solo cartoon.
Chuck Wichgers (born July 4, 1965) is a Republican member of the Wisconsin State Assembly since 2017.

The Diocese of Ypres was a Catholic diocese established in 1557 after the suppression of the Diocese of Thérouanne. It was later dissolved in 1801 and merged into the Diocese of Ghent. Jansenist theologian Cornelius Jansen served as its bishop from 1635 to 1638.
Sthenelaidas was a Spartan ephor in 432/431 BC who played a key role in pushing Sparta toward war with Athens, as recorded in Thucydides' *History of the Peloponnesian War*.

Nancy Duff Campbell (born ?) is an American lawyer and co-founder of the National Women's Law Center (NWLC). A former law professor, she joined Marcia Greenberger in 1978 to expand women's legal advocacy, leading to NWLC's establishment in 1981. Campbell litigated key cases on welfare rights and tax reform, including *Califano v. Westcott* (1979).

Peredur was a 6th-century warrior of the Hen Ogledd (Old North), son of Eliffer and brother of Gwrgi. He fought in the Battle of Arfderydd (573) and later appeared in Welsh legend, including the Triads and Y Gododdin. Geoffrey of Monmouth adapted him into Peredurus, a legendary British king, while the Middle Welsh romance Peredur son of Efrawg linked him to the Arthurian Percival.

Jack Wong Sue, OAM, DCM, JP (12 September 1925 – 16 November 2009), also known as Jack Sue(Chinese: 黄如彩), was a Chinese Australian from Perth, Western Australia. Wong Sue served as a member of the commando/special reconnaissance section, Z Special Unit, during the Second World War and was decorated with the Distinguished Conduct Medal. After the war, Wong Sue was a businessman, owning a diving store in the Perth suburb of Midland. He was also an author, a guide for tours of Borneo and a musician, who performed with bands in Perth for about 60 years.
Freerunner is a 2011 action film by Lawrence Silverstein starring Sean Faris, Danny Dyer and Tamer Hassan in lead roles.

Method cascading is a syntactic feature in object-oriented programming that allows multiple method calls on the same object without repeating the object reference. It is commonly used in fluent interfaces to simplify code by eliminating redundant expressions or temporary variables. Unlike method chaining, which evaluates to the result of each method, cascading evaluates to the original object. This feature appears in languages like Smalltalk (using semicolons), Visual Basic (with `With` blocks), and Dart (using double-dot `..` notation). Cascading improves readability and reduces verbosity in method-heavy operations.
Dar Hammuda Pasha is a historic palace in Tunis, built in 1630 by Muradid prince Hammuda Pasha Bey. It later served as a royal residence and was converted into a restaurant in 1957.

Ultimate Canada is the national governing body for the sport of ultimate (Frisbee) in Canada, overseeing leagues, tournaments, and national teams. Founded in 1993, it is affiliated with the World Flying Disc Federation (WFDF) and supports one of the world's largest ultimate communities. The sport was introduced to Canada in the early 1970s by Ken Westerfield, who helped establish the Toronto Ultimate Club (TUC) in 1980—Canada's first organized league. The first Canadian Ultimate Championships (CUC) were held in 1987, and Canada has since been a top-ranked nation in international competition.  

Ultimate Canada supports regional leagues such as the Vancouver Ultimate League, Ottawa-Carleton Ultimate Association, and Montreal Ultimate Association, among others. In 2013, Canada entered semi-professional ultimate with the Toronto Rush in the American Ultimate Disc League (AUDL), followed by teams in Montreal, Vancouver, and Ottawa. The sport gained further recognition in 2015 when the International Olympic Committee (IOC) granted full recognition to WFDF. Ultimate Canada also maintains a Hall of Fame to honor pioneers of the sport.
Greenwood Township is a civil township in St. Clair County, Michigan, established in 1855. As of the 2010 census, it had a population of 1,538.
Jean Garrabé (11 August 1931 – 13 September 2020) was a French psychiatrist.

Dimension (vector space)  

In mathematics, the dimension of a vector space is the number of vectors in any of its bases. It is a fundamental property that determines the space's structure and behavior. For example, the vector space ℝⁿ has dimension *n*, while the complex numbers ℂ have dimension 2 as a real vector space but dimension 1 as a complex vector space. The dimension is preserved under linear isomorphisms, meaning two finite-dimensional vector spaces over the same field are isomorphic if and only if they have the same dimension. Key results like the rank–nullity theorem relate dimension to linear maps. The concept generalizes to modules, matroids, and algebraic structures through trace-based definitions and Krull dimension in ring theory.

Spiders of New Zealand encompass a diverse range of species, including several unique and ecologically adapted spiders. Among the most notable are the fishing spiders of the genus *Dolomedes*, which hunt on the water's surface. Three species—*Dolomedes aquaticus*, *Dolomedes dondalei*, and *Dolomedes minor*—are widespread across New Zealand’s two main islands. *D. aquaticus* inhabits open riverbanks, while *D. dondalei* prefers forested streams. *D. minor*, the most common, is a nursery web spider that also exhibits fishing behavior. The largest species, *Dolomedes schauinslandi*, is found only on the Chatham Islands. New Zealand’s spider fauna includes many other species, contributing to the country’s rich biodiversity.

The Navy Midshipmen men's lacrosse team represents the United States Naval Academy in National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) Division I men's lacrosse. Navy currently competes as a member of the Patriot League and play their home games at Navy–Marine Corps Memorial Stadium in Annapolis, Maryland. During the 20th century, the Midshipmen secured 17 national championships, including 2 United States Intercollegiate Lacrosse Association titles and 15 Wingate Memorial Trophy awards. During the 1960s, a period of dominance for the Midshipmen, they won eight consecutive titles. The program's main rivals include Army, Maryland, and Johns Hopkins.
Ronald J. Horvath (born 1937) has made contributions to the study of colonialism, African development, and urban geography of Sydney, Los Angeles, Detroit and Addis Ababa.
Léon Spilliaert (1881–1946) was a Belgian symbolist and expressionist painter known for his melancholic, dreamlike works, including self-portraits and nocturnal seascapes.

Building officials are professionals responsible for ensuring compliance with construction codes and regulations. They may hold qualifications in engineering, architecture, or building surveying and are often licensed or certified. Their duties include reviewing plans, inspecting construction, and enforcing safety standards. In some jurisdictions, they work under a chief building official. Their role varies by country, with different regulatory frameworks in places like Australia, the U.S., and England.
The Rafz train crash occurred on 20 February 2015 in Switzerland when an InterRegio train collided with an S-Bahn train, injuring six people. The cause was suspected to be a signal violation (SPAD).

Muslim Community Radio (MCR) was a radio station based in London, United Kingdom founded in 1998. The station used to run through FM radio, at 87.7 FM (formerly 1143AM, 87.8 FM & 101.4 FM prior to that), and only broadcast annually during the holy month of Ramadan. The slogan of the radio station is 'Ramadan Special'.
Judith Révész (1915–2018) was a Hungarian-Dutch ceramicist and sculptor known for her earthenware and bronze works.
Kleiner Tiergarten is a 7-hectare urban park in Moabit, Berlin, established in 1655.

Stanstead is a village in Suffolk, England, located in the Babergh district. With a population of 319 (2011), it lies northeast of Sudbury in the Glem Valley. The name derives from the Anglo-Saxon for "stony place," reflecting its flint-strewn fields. The village has Iron Age and Roman settlement traces and is structured around Lower Street and Upper Street. It is served by Hartest CEVCP School and Sudbury Upper School.

A publicist is a professional who manages the public image and media relations of individuals, organizations, or brands. Their primary role is to secure favorable press coverage by crafting press releases, coordinating interviews, and maintaining relationships with journalists, producers, and bloggers. Publicists may work locally, nationally, or internationally, depending on their clients' needs. In the entertainment industry, they help celebrities navigate media exposure, manage crises, and maintain visibility. While some publicists focus on traditional media, others specialize in securing paid media deals for private individuals. The profession requires strong writing skills, networking abilities, and crisis management expertise. Publicists often hold degrees in communications, journalism, or public relations. Their salaries vary, with celebrity publicists typically earning more than corporate or local publicists.

Future of the Left are a Welsh alternative rock band based in Cardiff. The group consists of former Mclusky members Andrew Falkous (vocals, guitar) and Jack Egglestone (drums) and former Million Dead bassist Julia Ruzicka.

The Downs is a 412-acre (1.7 km²) public open space in Bristol, England, comprising Durdham Down (managed by Bristol City Council) and Clifton Down (owned by the Society of Merchant Venturers). Since 1861, they have been jointly managed for leisure, sports, and events.

Royalty Exchange is an American company that operates an online platform for buying and selling royalty assets of any type, mostly music, where royalty owners can sell their future payments to investors as alternative assets. The company hosts a centralized marketplace and online auction platform that connects a community of over 22,500+ investors with owners of royalty-based assets.

All types of assets can be monetized and invested in including music royalties, film royalties, and tax credits, patent and intellectual property royalties, trade secret royalties, renewable energy land leases, technology licensing royalties, book publishing royalties, franchise royalties, copyright royalties and agriculture royalties.

When Hope Calls is an American drama television series. The series is a spin-off of Hallmark Channel's When Calls the Heart, which is based on the Canadian West book series by Janette Oke. It premiered on August 30, 2019 as the inaugural original programming on Hallmark Movies Now, the Hallmark Channel's digital streaming service, with new episodes being released through October 25, 2019. The series was subsequently aired on Hallmark Channel in early 2020. The series was renewed for a second season and premiered on Great American Family on December 18, 2021.

Janet Haworth (born [year]) is a Welsh Conservative politician who served as a Member of the National Assembly for Wales for North Wales from 2015 to 2016, succeeding Antoinette Sandbach. Previously a Liberal Democrat, she joined the Conservatives in 2004. Haworth resigned as a councillor in 2015 amid criticism over poor attendance after becoming an AM.

"Dashboard" is a song by American indie rock band Modest Mouse and is the second track on their 2007 album We Were Dead Before the Ship Even Sank. The song was released as the first single from that album and peaked at number 5 on Billboard's Modern Rock Tracks chart. It debuted and peaked at #61 in the Billboard Hot 100 in early February 2007. In late May 2007, the song was released as a single in the United Kingdom with "King Rat" as the B-side. This single coincided with the band's UK tour. This song was #87 on Rolling Stone's list of the 100 Best Songs of 2007.

Members of Modest Mouse's e-mail list were sent a link of the finished track on January 3, 2007 and the song was released to American radio stations on January 16. The lyric, "The dashboard melted but we still have the radio" references Planes, Trains and Automobiles when Del (John Candy) is telling the police officer about the car after it is burned.

Lashkargah (Pashto/Persian: لشکرگاه), historically known as Bost, is the capital of Helmand Province in southern Afghanistan. Located along the Helmand River, it has a population of approximately 276,831 (2015 estimate). The city's name means "army barracks" in Persian, reflecting its origins as a Ghaznavid military outpost.  

Lashkargah became a modern urban center in the 1950s under the Helmand Valley Authority (HVA), a U.S.-backed irrigation project modeled after the Tennessee Valley Authority. The city features remnants of its medieval past, including the Ghaznavid fortress of Lashkari Bazar and the nearby Qala-e-Bost, known for its iconic arch.  

The city has faced significant conflict, including Taliban attacks in 2008 and 2020, before falling to the Taliban in August 2021. Despite instability, Lashkargah remains an agricultural and trade hub, supported by the Helmand River. It hosts educational institutions like Helmand University and media outlets such as Bost Television. Recent developments include infrastructure projects, though challenges like opium cultivation persist.
Southfield F.C. was an amateur English football club based in Birmingham, active from the 1880s to 1893. It competed in local cups and briefly entered the FA Cup.
James J. Carrigan (born April 25, 1941) is an American attorney and politician who served in the Massachusetts General Court.
Nashipur is a village in the Murshidabad-Jiaganj CD block in the Lalbag subdivision of Murshidabad district in West Bengal, India.
Ian Oswald (1929–2012) was a British psychiatrist and sleep researcher, known for his work on the effects of sleeping drugs.
Lev Abramovich Dodin (Lang-ru, born 1944) is a modern Russian theater director, the leader of Saint Petersburg Maly Drama Theater.

Wild Jack is a 1974 young adult science fiction novel written by the English prize-winning author John Christopher. Set in twenty-third century England, the plot concerns the young aristocrat Clive Anderson, who lives in decadent isolation within one of many walled-in hierarchal city-states, whose walls keep out the dangerous wilderness of the Outlands, and the barbaric savages known to hide there. When Clive is falsely accused of questioning the status quo, he is sent off to a brutal prison island. Faced with hunger and cruelty, he begins to truly question the good of his world.

The Yaquina River is a 59-mile (95 km) river in Benton and Lincoln counties in the U.S. state of Oregon. It originates in the Central Oregon Coast Range near Summit and flows west to its mouth at Yaquina Bay, near Newport, where it meets the Pacific Ocean. The river drains a watershed of 252 square miles (650 km²) and has an average discharge of 248 cubic feet per second (7.0 m³/s).  

The Yaquina River and its estuary support diverse fish populations, including salmon, steelhead, cutthroat trout, and sturgeon, while Yaquina Bay is home to species such as cabezon, rockfish, and crabs. Newport, located at the river's mouth, hosts a large charter fishing fleet targeting ocean species like halibut and salmon.  

The river has numerous tributaries, including the Little Yaquina River, Big Elk Creek, and Carlisle Creek, along with several sloughs such as Olalla Slough and Depot Slough. Public access is available via boat ramps along the lower river and bay.
Ian Thomas-Moore (born 26 August 1976) is an English former professional footballer who played as a striker or winger, notably for Tranmere Rovers and Burnley.

Te Mapoutahi or simply Mapoutahi is a peninsula on the coast of Otago, New Zealand, between the townships of Waitati and Pūrākaunui, within the limits of Dunedin City. It lies some 20 kilometres (12 mi) north of Dunedin's city centre.

Known also, confusingly, as "Goat Island", the peninsula's narrow isthmus and rugged coastline made it the perfect defensive location for a pā, a fortified settlement. The headland was, indeed, occupied by a Kāti Māmoe pā during the century before the arrival of Europeans in the early 1800s, and was the site of a major massacre in around 1750 when the warrior Taoka attacked the fortifications and killed those within.

The peninsula is now a scenic and historic reserve and is the site of a popular walking track.

The adjacent beach, Mapoutahi Cove, is used by naturists for nude walks and sunbathing. New Zealand has no official nude beaches, as public nudity is legal on any beach where it is "known to occur".

Woodall services is a motorway service area (MSA) on the M1 in South Yorkshire, England, operated by Welcome Break since 1968. Located near the Derbyshire border, it features fast-food outlets, a footbridge connecting both sides, and murals by David Fisher. Popular with coach travelers, it employs over 230 staff.

Boomplay or Boomplay Music is a media streaming and download service developed by Transsnet Music Limited. The service was first launched in Nigeria in 2015 by TECNO Mobile, Transsion Holdings. Boomplay has a freemium and subscription based service; basic features are free with advertisements or limitations, while additional features, such as download for offline play and ad-free listening are offered via paid subscriptions. The service is currently available for Web via www.boomplay.com, Android and iOS use. As of August 3, 2018, Boomplay Music recorded 10 million installations off the Google Play and App store. It currently has over 70 million monthly users (MAU) with a catalogue of over 85 million songs.

SLINEX (Sri Lanka India Naval Exercise) are a series of naval exercises between the Indian Navy and the Sri Lanka Navy. The first SLINEX exercise took place in 2005. The eighth edition took place in 2020.

The Great Lakes Depression (Lang-mn, Ikh Nuuruudyn Khotgor), also called the Great Lakes' Hollow, is a large semi-arid depression in Mongolia that covers parts of the Uvs, Khovd, Bayan-Ölgii, Zavkhan and Govi-Altai aimags. Bounded by the Altai in the West, Khangai in the East and Tannu-Ola Mountains in the North, it covers the area of over 100,000 km2 (39,000 sq mi) with elevations from 750 to 2,000 m (2,460–6,560 ft).

Small northern parts of the depression are part of Russia.

The depression is named so because it contains six major Mongolian lakes: saline Uvs Nuur, Khyargas Nuur and Dörgön Nuur; and freshwater Khar-Us Nuur, Khar Nuur and Airag Nuur, as well as a number of smaller ones. In addition, it includes 14,000 km2 (5,400 sq mi) of solonchaks and large sandy areas. Northern parts are dominated by arid steppes, and southern by semideserts or deserts. The major rivers are Khovd River, Zavkhan Gol, and Tesiin Gol.

The Commissioner of the Delhi Police is the head of the Delhi Police, appointed by the Ministry of Home Affairs. Established in 1978 under the Delhi Police Act, the commissioner holds the rank of Director General of Police. The current commissioner is Sanjay Arora, IPS, who assumed office on 1 August 2022.

Tauherenikau is a rural locality and a statistical area in the South Wairarapa District and Wellington Region of New Zealand's North Island. The locality is on SH 1 about 5 km east of Featherston and 7 km southwest of Greytown by road, and the statistical area covers the Tauherenikau River valley and plain north of SH 53. The statistical area surrounds but does not include Greytown.

The name, originally Tauwharenikau, means "the house made of nikau palm fronds".

Tauherenikau Racecourse opened in 1874.

Tauherenikau School operated from 1878 to 1936. It closed as the roll had dwindled to five students. The peak roll was 46 in 1917.

Fernside Homestead is a large wooden house built in 1924 on the west side of the river near Tauherenikau.

Natalia Maliszewska (born 16 September 1995) is a Polish short track speed skater. She won silver medal at the 2018 World Short Track Speed Skating Championships and gold medal at the 2019 European Short Track Speed Skating Championships.

Mickey Doyle is a fictional character in the HBO series *Boardwalk Empire*, portrayed by Paul Sparks. A Polish-American gangster and bootlegger, he operates in Atlantic City during Prohibition, frequently switching allegiances between crime bosses like Nucky Thompson and rival factions. Known for his opportunistic and often bumbling nature, Doyle plays a key role in several gang conflicts before being killed by Lucky Luciano in the final season. The character is loosely based on real-life mobster Mickey Duffy.

The Braille pattern dots-1456 (⠹) is a 6-dot braille cell used in unified international braille to represent the voiceless dental fricative (/θ/) or as needed. Related 8-dot patterns include 14567, 14568, and 145678. In Japanese *kantenji*, it corresponds to patterns 2568, 12568, 24568, and 124568.

FC Emmen is a Dutch football club based in Emmen, Drenthe. They play in the Eredivisie, the top tier of Dutch football, after winning the 2021–22 Eerste Divisie title. Founded in 1925, the club entered the professional Eerste Divisie in 1985. In 2018, Emmen were promoted to the Eredivisie for the first time, but in 2021 they were relegated to the Eerste Divisie again. 2022 they were promoted again and play the 2022-2023 season in the Eredivisie again. Home games are played at De Oude Meerdijk.

The 1965 Pakistani presidential election was held on 2 January 1965, in which incumbent President Ayub Khan defeated Fatima Jinnah, the sister of Pakistan's founder, Muhammad Ali Jinnah. The election was conducted under the Basic Democracy system, where 80,000 Basic Democrats served as an electoral college.  

Ayub Khan, who had ruled since the 1958 military coup, campaigned on his economic reforms and political stability. Fatima Jinnah, backed by the Combined Opposition Parties (COP), challenged his authoritarian rule but faced restrictions, including limited campaigning and clerical opposition to a female leader. Despite strong urban support, particularly in Karachi and Dhaka, Jinnah lost due to Ayub's rural dominance.  

Ayub won 62.43% of the electoral vote, while Jinnah secured 35.86%. The opposition alleged rigging, but protests faded after Jinnah accepted the results. The election marked a significant moment for women in Pakistani politics, encouraging greater female participation in later years.
John Barnard (1705–1784) was a British shipbuilder who constructed vessels for the Royal Navy and the East India Company.

Mapoutahi is a historic site in New Zealand associated with a significant 18th-century massacre between Māori tribes. According to oral tradition, Chief Taoka of Kāi Tahu sought revenge after his son was killed by his nephew (or cousin), Te Wera of Kāti Māmoe. After failing to capture Te Wera's pā at Huriawa, Taoka's war party attacked the undefended Mapoutahi Pā, slaughtering around 250 people. The aftermath was so devastating that the nearby settlement was named Pūrākaunui ("wood piled up"), referencing the stacked bodies. The site remains an important cultural landmark, reflecting the intertribal conflicts of pre-colonial Aotearoa.

Michael McClune (born August 22, 1989) is an American professional tennis player.

He turned professional in 2007, was represented by IMG and signed a lucrative contract with Nike.

*Two from Galilee* (1972) is a Christian novel by Marjorie Holmes that retells the biblical story of Mary and Joseph, from their betrothal to the birth of Jesus. Despite mixed critical reception, it became a bestseller and inspired a musical adaptation.

Climate change in the Gambia is having impacts on the natural environment and people of The Gambia. Like other countries in West Africa, the impacts of climate change are expected to be varied and complex. Climate change adaptation is going to be important to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals in the country.
Trisha Lynn Ford (née Dean; born October 19, 1977) is an American softball coach who is the current head coach at Texas A&M.

*Monarchs, Rulers, Dynasties and Kingdoms of the World* is a reference book that catalogs global dynasties and rulers throughout history. It includes a bibliography, a guide to dynasties and states, and brief historical descriptions of various realms, linking them to modern geography. The book helps identify major rulers and provides context on royal lineages and early kingdoms. While useful as a reference, some critics note its organizational complexity, making certain entries difficult to locate.

The fauna of Armenia includes diverse wildlife, with notable species such as the Persian leopard, a critically endangered big cat. Southern and southwestern Armenia serve as a key habitat for this species, with an estimated population of 10–20 individuals, connected to larger populations in Iran. Historically, the Asiatic lion once inhabited areas near Yerevan and the Araks River but was hunted to extinction in the region by the 10th century. Armenia's varied ecosystems support a range of other species, though habitat loss and human activity pose ongoing threats to its wildlife.

Poot Mae Nam Khong (Lang-th, RTGS: Phut Mae Nam Khong, literally The Spirit of Mekong River) is a Thai Horror Superstition Lakorn, remade from 1990 lakorn of the same name. The lakorn starring by Weir Sukollawat and Jui Warattaya as the Main roles and Morakot Aimee Kittisara as the ghost.

World Wide Adventures is a generalized name applied to Warner Bros. live-action short films of the 1960s. Usually, the trade magazines like BoxOffice only listed the one-reelers (running about 10 minutes in length) under this heading, with the longer films simply dubbed “specials.” For the most part, this was a handy marketing logo for a wide range of shorts of the documentary genre.

Oliver County is a county in the U.S. state of North Dakota. As of the 2020 census, its population was 1,877. The county seat and largest city is Center. Created in 1885, it is named after politician Harry S. Oliver. The county lies along the Missouri River and is part of the Bismarck metropolitan area.
George Ege (March 9, 1748 – December 14, 1829) was a United States Congressman, elected to the House of Representatives from Pennsylvania.
The Downs are an area of public open limestone downland in Bristol, England. They consist of Durdham Down to the north and east and Clifton Down to the south, separated by Stoke Road.

Eclecticism is the practice of selecting and borrowing ideas, styles, or methods from diverse sources rather than adhering to a single system or tradition. Originating in ancient Greek and Roman philosophy, it describes thinkers like Cicero who combined doctrines from Stoicism, Peripateticism, and Academic Skepticism. In architecture and art, eclecticism refers to the blending of elements from different historical styles, particularly in 19th-century design. The term also applies to martial arts that incorporate techniques from various disciplines, as well as textual criticism, where scholars compile readings from multiple manuscripts. In psychology, eclecticism involves integrating different therapeutic approaches. Modern philosophy and Middle Platonism further developed eclectic thought, emphasizing the synthesis of diverse philosophical traditions.
Bent Sæther (born 18 February 1969 in Oslo, Norway) is the bass guitarist and lead vocalist of the Norwegian psychedelic rock band Motorpsycho from the start in 1989.

Miniclip SA is a mobile game publisher and former browser game website that launched in 2001. It was started by Robert Small and Tihan Presbie with a budget of £40,000. In 2008, the company was valued at over £275 million.

In 2009, Miniclip hosted over 400 applications on its website.

In 2015, Tencent acquired majority stakes of Miniclip. In December 2016, Miniclip crossed 1 billion downloads across its mobile games on iOS, Android, and Windows devices. In March 2022, Miniclip announced that it had reached 4 billion downloads, with 8 Ball Pool alone accounting for 1 billion.

In April 2022, Miniclip announced that it would begin prioritizing its mobile games. As a result, the browser game portal was shut down in July 2022 and the website lost all but its two most popular games, Agar.io and 8 Ball Pool.

In June 2022, Miniclip acquired SYBO, the publisher of Subway Surfers, in an undisclosed deal.

2002 Euler is a stony background asteroid from the inner regions of the asteroid belt, approximately 17 kilometers (11 miles) in diameter. It was discovered on 29 August 1973, by Russian astronomer Tamara Smirnova at the Crimean Astrophysical Observatory in Nauchnyj, and assigned the prov. designation 1973 QQ1. It was named after Swiss mathematician Leonhard Euler.

Blaže Koneski (19 December 1921 – 7 December 1993) was a Macedonian poet, linguist, and scholar who played a key role in standardizing the Macedonian language. A professor at the University of Skopje, he authored influential works on Macedonian grammar and literature. He also served as president of the Macedonian Academy of Sciences and Arts. Koneski received numerous awards, including the Herder Prize, and remains a central figure in Macedonian cultural history.
Soman is a German electronic music project by Kolja Trelle, known for albums like *Sound Pressure* and *Mask*.

Typhoon Hagupit, known in the Philippines as Severe Tropical Storm Dindo, was a Category 1 typhoon that heavily impacted Eastern China and South Korea in August 2020. It was the fourth named storm and the second typhoon of the annual typhoon season. The JMA began monitoring a tropical depression that developed in the Philippine Sea on July 30, with PAGASA assigning the name “Dindo” to the storm, later that day it strengthened into a tropical storm, according to the JMA. PAGASA issued its final advisory on Dindo early on August 3, as it moved out of their area of responsibility. Hagupit intensified into a typhoon on August 3, before making landfall in Wenzhou, China at 19:30 UTC that day at peak intensity. Hagupit subsequently weakened over China, before degenerating into an extratropical low on August 5. Hagupit's remnants persisted for another several days, as the storm moved eastward, before dissipating south of Alaska on August 14.

Hagupit caused over a foot of rainfall in portions of Eastern China and South Korea. Hagupit caused 17 fatalities due to bringing heavy rains in the Korean Peninsula, and triggered a landslide which killed 6 people. Damage totals was reported to be at US$411 million.

The Fruitmarket Gallery is a contemporary art gallery in Edinburgh, Scotland, established in 1974 in a former fruit and vegetable market. It hosts exhibitions by international and Scottish artists, alongside public events. After a major refurbishment in 2021, it expanded its space and facilities, maintaining free admission.

Radio Dabanga (Arabic: راديو دبنقا) is a shortwave radio station, TV and online news magazine, that serves Sudan with daily reports on the latest political, economic and social information, including health issues and social programs such as Lost and Found. The shortwave radio has been broadcasting since 1 December 2008. Current broadcasts last for a total of one hour each day.

While Radio Dabanga only broadcast on shortwave radio for the first few years, the station now has several channels. The editors have innovated and experimented in the sphere of new media, often at the request of their listeners. These include Satellite TV, Facebook, WhatsApp, and SoundCloud. Depending on the medium, Radio Dabanga produces news in Sudanese Arabic and/or English.

Shortwave radio remains the mainstay of the radio station; one quarter of all Sudanese people are illiterate, a number that is relatively higher in Sudan’s remote areas and camps for displaced people. Many people here have no lifeline to the outside world other than telephone and radio, so it is Radio Dabanga’s mission to provide this.

Steilacoom (/ˈstɪləkəm/) is a town in Pierce County, Washington, United States. The population was 6,727 at the 2020 census. Steilacoom incorporated in 1854 and became the first incorporated town in what is now the state of Washington. It has also become a bedroom community for service members stationed at Joint Base Lewis–McChord.

Based on per capita income, Steilacoom ranks 61st of 522 areas ranked in the state of Washington.

KushCo Holdings (formerly Kush Bottles) is a publicly traded American company (OTCQB: KSHB) that provides packaging, containers, and ancillary products for the cannabis industry. Founded in 2010 in California, it expanded through acquisitions and now serves thousands of legal cannabis businesses nationwide.

In the Chicago mayoral election of 1923, Democrat William E. Dever defeated Republican Arthur C. Lueder and Socialist William A. Cunnea. Elections were held on April 3, the same day as aldermanic runoffs.

Sendiri (Alone in the Indonesian language) is a 1984 album by Indonesian singer Chrisye. It was his ninth studio album and eight with Musica Studios as well as his third album released in 1984 after Metropolitan and Nona (Miss).

Luro Administrative Post is a subdivision of Lautém Municipality in eastern East Timor. It covers 125.4 km² and had a population of 7,124 in 2015. The post consists of six *sucos*, including Afabubu and Baricafa, and is centered in Upper Luro. The economy relies on agriculture, particularly rice and corn, along with livestock farming. The area is known for its mountainous terrain and rivers such as the Adafuro and Roumoco.
Zati Sungur (1898–1984) was a Turkish magician known for his stage performances and innovations in illusion, including the "thin model sawing" trick.
Academic ranks in the United States are the titles, relative importance and power of professors, researchers, and administrative personnel held in academia.

Allobates femoralis (common name brilliant-thighed poison frog, brilliant-thighed poison-arrow frog) is a species of frog in the family Aromobatidae. It is found in Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, French Guiana, Guyana, Peru, and Suriname. Its natural habitat is tropical lowland forests.

The Additional Civil Lord of the Admiralty or formally the Office of the Additional Civil Lord of the Admiralty sometimes called the Department of the Additional Civil Lord of the Admiralty was a member of the Board of Admiralty first from 1882 to 1885 and then again from 1912 to 1919 who was mainly responsible for administration of contracts for matériel for the Fleet, supervision of the contracts and purchase department and general organisation of dockyards within the Admiralty.

The Braille pattern dots-1456 (⠹) is a 6-dot braille cell with both top, the middle right, and bottom right dots raised, or an 8-dot braille cell with both top, the upper-middle right, and lower-middle right dots raised. It is represented by the Unicode code point U+2839, and in Braille ASCII with a question mark:?.

*Year of the Gun* is a 1991 American thriller film directed by John Frankenheimer, starring Andrew McCarthy, Valeria Golino, and Sharon Stone. Set in 1970s Italy, it follows an American journalist whose novel draft about a political kidnapping is mistaken for a real conspiracy by the Red Brigades.

Kurihara (栗原市, Kurihara-shi) is a city located in Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. As of 30 April 2020, the city had an estimated population of 66,565, and a population density of 83 persons per km2 in 24,994 households. The total area of the city is 804.97 square kilometres (310.80 sq mi). Parts of the city are within the borders of the Kurikoma Quasi-National Park.
*Sprung* is an American crime comedy television series created by Greg Garcia, starring Garret Dillahunt, Martha Plimpton, and Phillip Garcia. It premiered on Amazon Freevee in 2022.
Hasan Zati Sungur (March 10, 1898 – July 6, 1984) was an internationally recognized Turkish stage magician.
The Rafz train crash occurred at approximately 6.43 am on 20 February 2015. An S-Bahn and an Interregio express train collided at Rafz railway station in Rafz, Canton of Zürich, Switzerland.

Vena (also known as D'Element and later DNA Latin Group) was an American bachata group formed in 2011 in the Bronx, New York. Originally consisting of Lenny Santos, Max Santos, and Steven Tejada (Steve Styles), the group emerged after the breakup of Aventura and Xtreme. They released hit singles like *"Señora"* and *"Dile a El"* before lineup changes led to their rebranding as DNA Latin Group in 2018. The group disbanded in 2019.

Paddleton is a 2019 American drama film directed by Alex Lehmann and starring Mark Duplass and Ray Romano. It follows two neighbors whose friendship is tested when one is diagnosed with terminal cancer and chooses assisted suicide. The film premiered at the Sundance Film Festival and was released on Netflix, receiving positive reviews for its heartfelt performances and emotional depth.
Two From Galilee is the first novel in a trilogy by Marjorie Holmes based on the life of Jesus Christ. The book was published in 1972.

Immune Therapy Holdings AB or ITH is a Swedish biotechnology R&D holding company headquartered at the Karolinska Institutet and Karolinska University Hospital in Stockholm.

ITH's research is primarily focused on its Tailored Leukapheresis (TLA) treatment for immune mediated inflammatory diseases (IMIDs).
Henry Monsang Tesong (born 28 February 1994) is an Indian professional footballer who plays as a defender for Sagolband United. He previously played for Pune and Chennai City in the I-League.

Ultimate Canada is a not-for-profit organization that serves as the governing body of the sport of Ultimate (also known as "Ultimate Frisbee") in Canada. It runs the Canadian Ultimate Championships (CUC) and Canadian University Ultimate Championship (CUUC) series.

Canadian Ultimate Championships (CUC)

Each August, teams from across the country travel to the Canadian Ultimate Championships (CUC) to compete for the national title in 7 different divisions: mixed, open, women's, junior open, junior women, masters open and masters women. Teams compete at this seven-day tournament not only to determine the national champion, but also to determine who will represent Canada at the next world championships.

From 2016 to 2019, the mixed divisions were held as a separate event from the remaining divisions.

In 2022, the format was changed again with the three Masters divisions at their own event, the three senior divisions at their own event, and the two Junior divisions at their own event.

Canadian University Ultimate Championships (CUUC)

The CUUC started in 1995 and brings university teams from across the country to compete in the open & the women's division. Each fall Ultimate Canada operates two competitions for university Ultimate teams in Canada: the Canadian University Ultimate Championships (CUUC) and the Canadian Eastern University Ultimate Championships (CEUUC). The CEUUC began in 1998 and brings university teams primarily from Ontario and Quebec together to compete in the open & the women's division.

Kalopanax septemlobus, common names castor aralia, tree aralia, and prickly castor oil tree, is a deciduous tree in the family Araliaceae, the sole species in the genus Kalopanax. It is native to northeastern Asia, from Sakhalin and Japan west to southwestern China. It is called cìqiū (刺楸) in Chinese, eumnamu (음나무) in Korean, and harigiri (ハリギリ; 針桐) in Japanese.

The Fruitmarket Gallery is a contemporary art gallery in Edinburgh, Scotland.

Since its opening in 1974, the gallery has become part of the Scottish contemporary art scene. After a period of closure to undergo a significant renovation, the gallery reopened in 2021 with expanded exhibition space and facilities.
The M class are a class of diesel-hydraulic locomotives built by Walkers Limited, Maryborough for the Western Australian Government Railways in 1972–1973.

Major General Hurdis Secundus Lalande Ravenshaw CMG (June 1869 – c. 6 June 1920) was a senior British Army officer in the First World War who served at the Royal Military College, Sandhurst and saw action on the North-West Frontier of India, in South Africa in the Second Boer War and in France and Greece in the First World War. In 1916 a German U-boat captured him, and he was a prisoner of war for the next two years. He died in 1920 in unusual circumstances after becoming lost in the South African bush near Port Elizabeth and succumbing to the elements.

Buttlejorrk is a rural locality in Victoria, Australia, historically significant as a staging post during the 1850s gold rush. Located near Aitken's Gap, it was a bustling stopover for travelers heading to the goldfields, featuring inns, stores, and a police station. The area declined after the Bendigo railway opened in 1859, though remnants such as a row of Monterey pines along the Calder Highway remain. A bluestone lock-up from the gold rush era was relocated to Sunbury in 1992 for preservation. Buttlejorrk also faced bushfires, including a major blaze in 1944 that destroyed properties in the region. Today, it is part of Victoria's historical landscape, with archaeological traces of its gold rush past.

Chester Creek is a 17.1-mile (27.5 km) stream in southeastern Pennsylvania, formed by the East and West Branches, which converge near Lenni before flowing into the Delaware River. It has historical significance as an early industrial site and remains ecologically healthy compared to nearby waterways.

Deccani Architecture refers to the architectural styles developed during the Deccan sultanate period. The Deccan sultanates were five dynasties that ruled late medieval kingdoms, namely, Bijapur, Golkonda, Ahmadnagar, Bidar, and Berar in south-western India. The Deccan sultanates were located on the Deccan Plateau. Their architecture was a regional variant of Indo-Islamic architecture, heavily influenced by the styles of the Delhi Sultanate and later Mughal architecture, but sometimes also directly from Persia and Central Asia.

The rulers of five Deccan sultanates had a number of cultural contributions to their credit in the fields of art, music, literature and architecture. Deccan sultanates have constructed many grand and impregnable forts. Bidar and Golconda forts are classic example of military planning of Deccan sultanates. Apart from forts, they have constructed many tombs, mosques and madrasas. Gol Gumbaz (tomb of Mohammed Adil Shah), was the second largest dome in the world.

In 2014, UNESCO put a group of buildings on its "tentative list" to become a World Heritage Site under the name Monuments and Forts of the Deccan Sultanate (despite there being a number of different sultanates). These are:

Henry Hastings, 3rd Earl of Huntingdon (1535 – 14 December 1595) was an English nobleman, administrator, and military commander. A descendant of the Plantagenets, he was educated alongside Edward VI and later married Katherine Dudley, daughter of the Duke of Northumberland. His support for Lady Jane Grey’s claim to the throne briefly led to his imprisonment under Mary I. Under Elizabeth I, he became a trusted Protestant loyalist, serving as President of the Council of the North (1572–1595) to counter Catholic threats, including Mary, Queen of Scots. Despite his royal lineage, his Yorkist ancestry hindered his political ambitions. He died in York, heavily in debt, and was buried at St Helen’s Church, Ashby-de-la-Zouch.

Akumaa Mama Zimbi, also known professionally as Dr. Joyce Akumaa Dongotey-Padi, is a Ghanaian television and radio broadcast journalist, women's rights activist, blogger, marriage counselor, and an actress in Ghana. Akumaa is known for her fascinating, yet sexually stimulating, ways of expressing "Medaase" (Thank you). Her exceptional ways of wearing her headgear also comes off as a distinct Akumaa brand. Little is known about Akumaa's early life as well as her family and educational background. However, it is known that her father is a policeman and her husband is a businessman to whom she has been married for over twenty-four years

The Port of Gaza is a small port near the Rimal district of Gaza City. It is the home port of Palestinian fishing-boats and the base of the Palestinian Naval Police, a branch of the Palestinian National Security Forces. Under the Oslo II Accord, the activities of the Palestinian Naval Police are restricted to 6 nautical miles from the coast. Since 2007, the Port of Gaza has been under an Israeli-imposed naval blockade as part of a blockade of the Gaza Strip, and activities at the port have been restricted to small-scale fishing.
Norway 1985 is a computer wargame published in October 1985 by Strategic Simulations. Developed by Roger Keating, it was the fourth in the "When Superpowers Collide" series.

Medelpad (IPA-sv or IPA-sv) is a historical province or landskap in the north of Sweden. It borders Hälsingland, Härjedalen, Jämtland, Ångermanland and the Gulf of Bothnia.

The province is a part of Norrland and as such considered to be Northern Sweden, although the province geographically is located in the middle of Sweden. It is a common misconception that the name Medelpad ("middle land" or "middle ground") reflects this, but the name actually refers to the fact that most of the province lies between its two rivers Ljungan and Indalsälven.

The Additional Civil Lord of the Admiralty was a temporary position on the British Board of Admiralty, active from 1882 to 1885 and 1912 to 1919. The role was typically held by a civilian expert rather than a naval officer or politician. Initially created for George W. Rendel, a shipbuilding engineer, it was later expanded during World War I to include multiple civil lords. Responsibilities included overseeing ship construction, repairs, and dockyard administration, often in coordination with the Third Sea Lord. The office was abolished in 1919.
Linda Gottlieb is an American television writer and film producer.

Mordaunt Bisset (died 1884) was a British landowner, politician, and Master of the Devon and Somerset Staghounds. He served as MP for West Somerset (1880–1883) and played a key role in reviving stag hunting on Exmoor. He inherited estates in Scotland and Wiltshire.

The 1960 Louisiana gubernatorial election was held on April 19, 1960, to elect the governor of Louisiana. As the Democratic Party dominated state politics, the primary runoff between Jimmie Davis and deLesseps Morrison determined the likely winner. Davis, backed by segregationist forces, defeated Morrison, who relied on urban and African-American support. In the general election, Davis easily defeated Republican Francis Grevemberg, marking the decline of the Long political faction and the rise of race as a central campaign issue.

JD Eicher (born July 27, 1986) is an American singer, songwriter, producer, and novelist from the Youngstown, Ohio area.

Both billed as JD Eicher & the Goodnights and independently as JD Eicher, Eicher has released nine albums—one of which also featured a Deluxe Edition—as well as a live EP and vinyl collection of his most popular songs.

Eicher also created an EP of his music to accompany the release of Nicholas Sparks' 20th novel called Two by Two. The musician and the author toured the country to promote this project.

Touring mostly in the United States, Eicher has a strong following in many regions of the nation. Eicher also occasionally tours in Europe and has fans in many cities abroad. Additionally, a number of Eicher's songs receive radio airplay and have been used in businesses, television ad campaigns, and television shows.
St John's Rectory is at 3 Vicar's Lane, Chester, Cheshire, England. It is recorded in the National Heritage List for England as a designated Grade II* listed building.
Ian Oswald (4 August 1929 – 25 April 2012) was a sleep researcher and psychiatrist.
R (Corner House Research) v Director of the Serious Fraud Office UKHL 60 is a UK constitutional law case, concerning the rule of law.
Raymond Duchamp-Villon (5 November 1876 – 9 October 1918) was a French sculptor.

New Zealand has 1157 described spider species, with an estimated total fauna of 2000 species. Over 97 per cent are endemic, and the rest have been introduced through human activities or were natural wind-borne introductions.

The New Zealand spider with the largest leg span is the Nelson cave spider (Spelungula cavernicola), with a leg span of up to 13 centimetres (5.1 in) and a 3 centimetres (1.2 in) body length.

The Australian white-tailed spider, first recorded in New Zealand in 1886, has been falsely attributed as the cause of many necrotising spider bites.

The flat huntsman spider (Delena cancerides), also from Australia, and called the Avondale spider in New Zealand, was accidentally introduced in the early 1920s, possibly in shipments of hardwood logs used for railway sleepers. The huntsman spiders, which are considered harmless to humans, have been collected for use in at least two films.

Very few New Zealand spiders have bites that can cause significant injury to humans, and of these, only one – the katipō – is endemic. Katipō bites have been known to cause systemic effects, such as hypertension, seizure, or coma, though no deaths as a result of katipō bites have been recorded for over 200 years. Its more dangerous close relative, the venomous Australian redback spider, has established a foothold in some parts of New Zealand, notably in Taranaki and Central Otago.

The eCRM or electronic customer relationship management coined by Oscar Gomes encompasses all standard CRM functions with the use of the net environment i.e., intranet, extranet and internet. Electronic CRM concerns all forms of managing relationships with customers through the use of information technology (IT).

eCRM processes include data collection, data aggregation, and customer interaction. Compared to traditional CRM, the integrated information for eCRM intraorganizational collaboration can be more efficient to communicate with customers.

Immune Therapy Holdings is a privately held Swedish biotech company, founded in 2005, specializing in immunotherapy. It developed Tailored Leukapheresis (TLA), an apheresis treatment targeting pro-inflammatory cells. The company has received awards and research funding for its innovation.

The Mercedes-Benz W140 is a series of flagship vehicles manufactured by Mercedes-Benz from 1991 to 1998 in sedan/saloon and coupe body styles and two wheelbase lengths (SE and SEL). Mercedes-Benz unveiled the W140 S-Class at Geneva Motor Show in March 1991 with sales launch in April 1991 and North American launch on 6 August 1991.

All models were renamed in June 1993 as part of the corporate-wide nomenclature changes for 1994 model year on, becoming S regardless of wheelbase length or body style as well as fuel type. Diesel models carried a TURBODIESEL trunk/boot lid label. In 1996, the S-Class coupé was renamed again as CL-Class into its own model range.

The W140 series S-Class was superseded by the W220 S-Class sedan and C215 CL-Class coupé in 1998 after an eight-year production run.

Production of the W140 reached 432,732, with 406,710 sedans and 26,022 coupés.
The alternative Christmas message is a message broadcast by Channel 4 since 1993, as a sometimes humorous and sometimes serious alternative to the traditional Royal Christmas Message.
Chuck Wichgers (born July 4, 1965) is a Wisconsin politician and businessman. He is a Republican.

Curtis Knight and the Squires were an American R&B band active in the 1960s, best known for featuring guitarist Jimi Hendrix from 1965 to 1966. Their recordings, including *Hornet's Nest* and *Knock Yourself Out*, later gained attention due to Hendrix's involvement.

Jack Ross (born 6 November 1962 in Auckland), is a New Zealand poet, novelist, translator, editor and lecturer in creative writing. A trilogy of novels has been published by two different publishers, Alan Brunton's Bumper Books (Volume 1) and Titus Books.

Raquel Torres Cerdán (born November 19, 1948) is a Mexican chef and anthropologist known for preserving and promoting the traditional cuisine of Veracruz. She owns restaurants dedicated to regional foods and teaches workshops on Mexican culinary heritage.

The kidnapping of Alexandra Măceșanu and Luiza Melencu occurred in Romania in 2019. Fifteen-year-old Alexandra was abducted while hitchhiking and called emergency services from her captor's house, but police delayed their response. Her kidnapper, Gheorghe Dincă, confessed to killing her and 18-year-old Luiza, who had disappeared months earlier. The case sparked national outrage over police incompetence and raised suspicions of a human trafficking cover-up. Protests led to high-level resignations, including the interior minister. Both girls' families dispute the official investigation.

Caguas, Puerto Rico is a city and municipality in the central-eastern region of Puerto Rico. Founded in 1775, it is nicknamed *"La Ciudad del Turabo"* (Turabo Valley) and *"La Cuna de los Trovadores"* (Cradle of Trovadores). Located in the fertile Caguas Valley, it is the fifth-most populous municipality on the island, with 127,244 residents as of 2020.  

Historically an agricultural hub, Caguas was a major sugarcane producer and later became a pharmaceutical manufacturing center. It is home to landmarks such as the Caguas Cathedral and the Jardín Botánico William Miranda Marín. The city hosts the *Criollos de Caguas*, a storied baseball team with multiple Caribbean Series titles.  

Caguas is governed by Mayor William Miranda Torres (PPD) and is part of Puerto Rico's Senatorial District VII. Despite its inland location, it functions as a commuter town for San Juan. The city has faced challenges such as Hurricane Maria (2017) and economic shifts following the expiration of federal tax incentives.  

Transportation relies heavily on cars, with highways connecting it to San Juan and eastern Puerto Rico. Caguas is twinned with Hartford, Connecticut, and Southbridge, Massachusetts.

The Port of Gaza is a small harbor in the Gaza Strip, operated by the Palestinian National Authority. Historically linked to the ancient port of Maiuma, it has faced restrictions due to the Israeli blockade imposed since 2007. The port has been deepened and expanded by Hamas, but plans for a larger seaport under the Oslo Accords remain unrealized. Israeli forces have previously attacked the port, and access remains limited to small fishing boats. The port has also been the focus of attempts to break the blockade, including activist flotillas.

Pope Benedict V (died 4 July 965) was Bishop of Rome from 22 May to 23 June 964 during a conflict between the Holy Roman Emperor Otto I and the Roman nobility. Elected after the death of Pope John XII, he was deposed by Otto, who reinstated Pope Leo VIII. Benedict was exiled to Hamburg, where he died. His remains were later returned to Rome.

The kidnapping of Alexandra Măceșanu and Luiza Melencu happened in Caracal, Olt County, Romania on 25 July 2019. Romanian police were heavily criticized for taking 19 hours to locate and search the building where she was held captive. The handling of the case met widespread outrage and protests, which led to the dismissal of Interior Minister Nicolae Moga and other officials.

William Henry Seaman (November 15, 1842 – March 8, 1915) was an American lawyer, politician, and federal judge. A Democrat, he served as mayor of Sheboygan, Wisconsin, before being appointed to the U.S. District Court (1893–1905) and later the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Seventh Circuit (1905–1915). He was a Union Army veteran of the Civil War.

The White Obelisk is a four-sided limestone monument from ancient Assyria, dating to 1050–1031 BC. Discovered in Nineveh (modern Iraq) by Hormuzd Rassam in 1853, it is now housed in the British Museum. Standing 284 cm tall, it features detailed relief carvings depicting military campaigns, royal hunts, and religious ceremonies, possibly under King Ashurnasirpal I. An inscription at the top describes the king’s conquests and offerings to the goddess Ishtar. The obelisk is one of the earliest known examples of Assyrian monumental art.

Luro, officially Luro Administrative Post (Lang-pt, Lang-tet), is an administrative post (and was formerly a subdistrict) in Lautém municipality, East Timor; Luro is also the name of two of its towns (Upper and Lower Luro).

The main river of the administrative post is the Adafuro, running between Wairoque and Cotamutu and through Afabubu. Principal crops grown in the subdistrict include corn, rice, coconuts, cassava and other vegetables.

Collar × Malice is a visual novel otome game developed by Idea Factory under their Otomate brand. Released in Japan in 2016 for PlayStation Vita and later ported to Nintendo Switch, it follows rookie police officer Ichika Hoshino, who is forced to investigate a terrorist group called Adonis after being fitted with a poisoned collar. The game features multiple romanceable characters, including former police officers aiding her investigation. It received mixed reviews but was praised for its suspenseful plot and character dynamics. A fan disc, *Collar × Malice: Unlimited*, and an anime film adaptation (set for 2023) were later announced.

Jemima Morrell (7 March 1832 – 13 October 1909) was an English diarist and traveler best known for her detailed journal documenting the first guided tour of Switzerland in 1863, organized by Thomas Cook. Born in Selby, Yorkshire, she was part of a middle-class family that enabled her education and travel. Her lively account, *Miss Jemima's Swiss Journal*, provides a rare glimpse into early mass tourism, Victorian travel customs, and the experiences of women adventurers. After her marriage to landowner John Broadley Greenwood in 1867, she settled in Yorkshire. Her journal, rediscovered during World War II, was published in 1963 and later inspired commemorative tours and literary works.
Bethuel Muzeu (born 22 February 2000) is a Namibian professional footballer who plays as a forward for Black Leopards and the Namibia national team.

An ultrasonic motor is a type of electric motor that uses high-frequency vibrations (typically ultrasonic, above 20 kHz) to generate motion. Unlike traditional electromagnetic motors, it relies on friction modulation between a vibrating stator and a rotor to produce precise, controlled movement. Ultrasonic motors operate using either traveling-wave or standing-wave vibrations, with the former allowing bidirectional motion and higher efficiency.  

These motors are known for their high torque at low speeds, compact size, and quiet operation, making them ideal for applications requiring precision, such as autofocus lenses in cameras (e.g., Canon's USM technology). They can function at micro- or nano-scale displacements, depending on frequency, and are used in consumer electronics, medical devices, and robotics. Some designs even employ near-field acoustic levitation for non-contact motion.

Bent Sæther (born 18 February 1969) is a Norwegian musician, composer, and producer, best known as the bassist and vocalist of the rock band Motorpsycho. He also founded The International Tussler Society and has produced albums for notable Norwegian bands.

BBC Radio Wiltshire is the BBC's local radio station serving the English county of Wiltshire. It broadcasts on FM, DAB, digital TV and via BBC Sounds from studios at Prospect Place in Swindon.

According to RAJAR, the station had a weekly audience of 64,000 and a 1.6% share as of September 2022.

The Technological Educational Institute of Peloponnese (Lang-el; formerly Technological Educational Institute of Kalamata, Τεχνολογικό Εκπαιδευτικό Ίδρυμα Καλαμάτας, TEIKAL) was one of the 15 Technological Educational Institutes in Greece. Its main campus and administrative centre was located in Kalamata and one department was located in Sparta.
London Bourne (1793–1869) was a former Barbadian slave who became a wealthy merchant and abolitionist.

John Graham (born 1954–1957) is a Canadian Indigenous activist and convicted murderer, known for his involvement in the American Indian Movement (AIM) and the 1975 killing of fellow activist Anna Mae Aquash.

Medelpad is a historical province in northern Sweden, part of the Norrland region. It lies within Västernorrland County and covers 7,058 km², with a population of around 125,000. The province is bordered by the Ljungan and Indalsälven rivers and features the UNESCO-listed High Coast, known for its post-glacial rebound. The largest city, Sundsvall, was historically its only town. Medelpad's coat of arms symbolizes its river-divided landscape. The region is known for football (GIF Sundsvall) and ice hockey (Timrå IK).
Samuel Lord (d. May 23, 1889) was an English-born American merchant who co-founded the department store Lord & Taylor in New York City in 1826. After retiring in 1862, he returned to England.

Substance Church is an evangelical megachurch in Minneapolis, Minnesota, founded in 2004 by Pastor Peter Haas. Known for its youthful congregation and contemporary worship style—including electronic music and multimedia production—it operates multiple campuses. Substance emphasizes church planting and cell group ministry while blending evangelical theology with charismatic influences. The church gained recognition for its innovative approach, including music projects and social media engagement.
Kate Addo (born 23 July 1972) is a Ghanaian journalist, broadcaster, and public relations practitioner. She is the Director of Public Affairs at the Parliament of Ghana.

Gemeinhardt is an American flute and piccolo manufacturer based in Elkhart, Indiana. Founded in 1948 by German flute-maker Kurt Gemeinhardt, the company initially produced professional flutes before expanding into student models. Known for its beginner flutes, Gemeinhardt also manufactures conservatory and professional instruments, including alto and bass flutes. Since 2011, it has been owned by Taiwan-based Angel Industries, though key components are still made in the U.S. The company also produces piccolos, including those under the Roy Seaman brand.

Sendiri is the sixth studio album by Indonesian singer Chrisye, released in 1984. Produced with Addie MS, it marked a return to romantic pop after his art rock phase. The album spawned three hit singles and won the 1984 BASF Award for best-selling album.

Jun Nishida (1977–2005) was a Japanese ceramic artist known for his avant-garde sculptural works. A graduate of Kyoto Seika University, he gained recognition for his *Zetsu* series, created through an intense process of firing, excavation, and fracturing of porcelain and glaze. His radical approach challenged traditional ceramic techniques, emphasizing organic, unpredictable forms. Nishida exhibited widely in Japan and won international awards, including the Grand Prix at the 53rd Faenza Biennale. He died in a kiln explosion in Indonesia at age 28. His works are held in major museums worldwide.

Akumaa Mama Zimbi is a Ghanaian actress, radio and television host, and philanthropist. Rising to fame in the 1990s through her role in the *Cantata Show*, she later transitioned into radio, hosting programs such as *Odo Ahomaso*, where she offers relationship and marital advice. Known as "#IAmTheSexDoctor" and a "Relationship Doctor," she is a prominent voice on love and marriage in Ghana. Through her Mama Zimbi Foundation, established in 2004, she supports widows and educates youth on sexual health. She has won awards, including Radio Development Show Host of the Year (2019–2020).

Larissa Takchi (born 1997) is an Australian television cook and winner of *MasterChef Australia* in 2019. Of Lebanese descent, she gained fame for her winning dish, a Szechuan-inspired pavlova, and later became a food columnist.

The Armenia Marriott Hotel Yerevan is a luxury hotel in Yerevan, Armenia, managed by Marriott International. Opened in 1958 as the Hotel Armenia, it was the largest hotel in the Armenian SSR and originally operated by the Soviet state-run Intourist agency. Designed by architects Mark Grigorian and Eduard Sarapyan, it was built with pink Armenian tufa stone. After privatization in 1998, it underwent a $40 million renovation and reopened in 1999 under Marriott. The hotel features 144 rooms, 23 suites, four restaurants, conference halls, a fitness center, and an outdoor pool. Located near Republic Square, it remains a prominent landmark in Yerevan.

The Greek Motorway 5 (Lang-el; code: A5) is a motorway in Greece. The motorway, commonly referred to as Ionia Odos (Lang-el) or Ionia Motorway, starts at Ioannina and it follows the western coastline of mainland Greece down to the Gulf of Corinth. At Rio, it crosses the gulf via the Rio–Antirrio bridge and is connected with the A8 Motorway at an interchange near Patras. The future, currently under construction, Patras - Pyrgos motorway is typically part of the A5 Motorway.

In August 2017, the last section under construction (Perdika-Ioannina) was completed and delivered to traffic by the Greek Minister of Infrastructure, Transport and Network, making Ionia Odos a fully operational motorway. It is the second major north–south road connection after Motorway 1 and is also part of the trans-balkanic Adriatic–Ionian motorway and the European routes E55 and E951.

The Ionia Odos motorway was one of the most challenging construction projects in Greece, as it spans a big part of Western Greece and because of its location on the foothills of the Pindus mountain range. After its completion in August 2017, it reduced travel times between Antirrio and Ioannina to 1 hour and 40 minutes, down from 3 hours and 30 minutes and will provide a boost to the economy of the regions it spans (Western Greece and Epirus).

Jun Nishida (西田潤, Nishida Jun, 1977 – March 26, 2005) was a Japanese ceramicist. He is best known for his massive conceptual pottery pieces, which experiment with the material capacities of clay and the imaginative forms that ceramics could take amid the intense thermochemical conditions of the kiln. Throughout his brief yet productive career, Nishida pushed the boundaries of contemporary ceramics, challenging conventions of scale, abstraction, and method to produce a radically new visual language of pottery.

Final Account is a 2020 German-language documentary film directed and produced by Luke Holland, who died shortly after post-production was completed on June 10, 2020. The film follows the last living generation of German participants in Adolf Hitler's Third Reich.

Final Account premiered at the 77th Venice International Film Festival on September 2, 2020. It was released on May 21, 2021, by Focus Features.

'(born 1973) is a German political scientist, lawyer and politician of the Christian Democratic Union of Germany (CDU). A resident of Belgium, he is an official of the Secretariat of the European Parliament. As a political scientist, he is known as a student of Eckhard Jesse in the tradition of German extremism research, and has published works on the political tradition of the former Party of Democratic Socialism. He was one of the leading figures within the Junge Union during the early 2000s and is currently chairman of the Brussels branch of the CDU, the party's only branch outside Germany. He ran unsuccessfully as a CDU candidate for the Bundestag in the 2005 German federal election.

The Allied Commission was a series of multinational bodies established after World War II to oversee the implementation of armistice terms and occupation policies in defeated Axis countries. These commissions operated in Italy, Romania, Finland, Bulgaria, Hungary, Germany, Austria, and Japan, ensuring compliance with Allied demands, including demilitarization, political reforms, and war crime trials. While nominally representing multiple Allied powers, some commissions—particularly in Eastern Europe—were dominated by the Soviet Union, facilitating communist influence. The commissions were gradually dissolved as peace treaties were ratified, with the last, the Allied Control Council for Germany, disbanding in 1991 after German reunification.

Ultrasound-modulated optical tomography (UOT) is a form of tomography involving ultrasound. It is used in imaging of biological soft tissues and has potential applications for early cancer detection. Like optical techniques, this method provides high contrast, and the use of ultrasound also provides high resolution.

Jatavarman Sundara I, also known as Sadayavarman Sundara Pandyan, was a emperor of the Pandyan dynasty who ruled regions of Tamilakkam (present day South India) between 1250–1268 CE. He is remembered for his patronage of the arts and tamil architecture, along with refurbishment and decoration of many Kovils (temple) in the Tamil continent. He oversaw a massive economic growth of the Pandyan empire. On the eve of his death in 1268 CE, the second Pandyan empire's power and territorial extent had risen to its zenith.
Chester Creek is a 9.4-mile-long (15.1 km) tributary of the Delaware River in Delaware County, Pennsylvania in the United States.

The pluperfect (shortening of plusquamperfect), usually called past perfect in English, is a type of verb form, generally treated as a grammatical tense in certain languages, relating to an action that occurred prior to an aforementioned time in the past. Examples in English are: "we had arrived"; "they had written".

The word derives from the Latin plus quam perfectum, "more than perfect". The word "perfect" in this sense means "completed"; it contrasts with the "imperfect", which denotes uncompleted actions or states.

In English grammar, the pluperfect (e.g. "had written") is now usually called the past perfect, since it combines past tense with perfect aspect. (The same term is sometimes used in relation to the grammar of other languages.) English also has a past perfect progressive (or past perfect continuous) form: "had been writing".

Emerich (Imre) Vogl (born 12 August 1905 in Temesvár, Austria-Hungary (now Romania) – died 29 October 1971 in Bucharest, Romania) was a Romanian football player and coach of Banat Swabian ethnicity who was a member of Romanian team which participated at the 1930 FIFA World Cup in Uruguay and 1934 FIFA World Cup in Italy.

Jagadishpur is a census town in the Howrah district of West Bengal, India. Located along Benaras Road (State Highway 15), it has a population of 16,259 (2011 Census). The nearest railway station is Kona on the Howrah-Amta line. The town is part of the Domjur Vidhan Sabha constituency.
